question: In Wix, how do you add an 'Accessibility Policy' section to your webpage?
answer: Click the 'Add Section' button in the center of the editor interface. Then select 'Accessibility Policy' from the dropdown menu that appears, which adds a new 'Accessibility' section to your layout.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Add Section' button located in the center of the Wix editor interface.
A dropdown menu appears, displaying various section options including 'Accessibility Policy'.
The user selects the 'Accessibility Policy' section from the list, and a new section titled 'Accessibility' is added to the webpage layout.

question: In Wix, how do you modify both the image and text content in an Accessibility Policy section?
answer: Click the 'Edit & Replace' button under the image placeholder to change the default image. Then type your content directly into the 'Paragraph' field within the editable area of the section.
keypoints: The screen displays an editable area with placeholders for title, subtitle, paragraph, media, and buttons within the 'Accessibility' section.
The user clicks on the 'Edit & Replace' button under the image placeholder to modify the default image.
The user types text into the 'Paragraph' field, updating the content of the accessibility policy description.

question: In Wix, what steps change the design layout of the Accessibility section to left-aligned?
answer: First click the 'Design' tab at the top of the editor. Then scroll through the design templates and select a left-aligned layout option, which automatically applies the new visual arrangement to the section.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Design' tab at the top of the Wix editor to access design customization options for the section.
The user scrolls through different design templates for the 'Accessibility' section, selecting a left-aligned layout option.
The selected design template is applied, altering the visual arrangement of the section elements on the page.
